message.content:
Google Lighthouse’s **Performance** category currently contains **38 audits** in total: **6 metric audits**, **22 opportunities**, and **10 diagnostics**.[1][5] Below is the complete set of audits in that category, with the **exact audit ID**, **human-readable title**, **what it measures/verifies**, and **audit type**.

> Note: Lighthouse’s **metrics** in the Performance category are the six user-facing performance metrics **FCP, LCP, TBT, CLS, SI, and TTI**.[2][3][4]

| Audit ID | Title | Measures / verifies | Type |
|---|---|---|---|
| `first-contentful-paint` | First Contentful Paint | Time until the first text or image is painted on screen. | Metric |
| `largest-contentful-paint` | Largest Contentful Paint | Time until the largest visible content element is rendered. | Metric |
| `speed-index` | Speed Index | How quickly the page’s visible content is populated during load. | Metric |
| `cumulative-layout-shift` | Cumulative Layout Shift | Amount of unexpected layout shift during page load. | Metric |
| `total-blocking-time` | Total Blocking Time | Total time the page is blocked from responding to user input during loading. | Metric |
| `interactive` | Time to Interactive | Time until the page is considered fully interactive. | Metric |

| Audit ID | Title | Measures / verifies | Type |
|---|---|---|---|
| `uses-responsive-images` | Properly size images | Images are not larger than necessary for their rendered display size. | Opportunity |
| `offscreen-images` | Defer offscreen images | Images below the fold are deferred/lazy-loaded instead of loaded immediately. | Opportunity |
| `uses-optimized-images` | Efficiently encode images | Image formats and encoding are efficient for file size. | Opportunity |
| `uses-webp-images` | Serve images in next-gen formats | Images could be served in newer formats such as WebP/AVIF where supported. | Opportunity |
| `uses-text-compression` | Enable text compression | Text resources are compressed in transit. | Opportunity |
| `render-blocking-resources` | Eliminate render-blocking resources | CSS/JS that delay first render are identified for deferral/removal. | Opportunity |
| `unminified-css` | Minify CSS | CSS is not unnecessarily large due to lack of minification. | Opportunity |
| `unminified-javascript` | Minify JavaScript | JavaScript is not unnecessarily large due to lack of minification. | Opportunity |
| `unused-css-rules` | Remove unused CSS | CSS rules are loaded but not used by the page. | Opportunity |
| `unused-javascript` | Reduce unused JavaScript | JavaScript is loaded but not used on initial page load. | Opportunity |
| `uses-long-cache-ttl` | Serve static assets with an efficient cache policy | Static resources do not use an effective long-lived cache. | Opportunity |
| `efficient-animated-content` | Avoid large layout shifts | Animated content is likely causing layout instability. | Opportunity |
| `modern-image-formats` | Serve images in next-gen formats | Opportunity to reduce image payload by using modern formats. | Opportunity |
| `network-server-latency` | Reduce initial server response time | Server response time is high and could be improved. | Opportunity |
| `mainthread-work-breakdown` | Minimize main-thread work | Main-thread work during load is heavy and should be reduced. | Opportunity |
| `bootup-time` | Reduce JavaScript execution time | JS parse/eval/execution time during startup is high. | Opportunity |
| `font-display` | Ensure text remains visible during webfont load | Webfonts may be causing invisible text while loading. | Opportunity |
| `diagnostics`-style audit `dom-size`? | Avoid an excessive DOM size | The DOM is very large, which can hurt performance. | Opportunity |
| `third-party-summary` | Reduce impact of third-party code | Third-party scripts/resources are contributing to page cost. | Opportunity |
| `document-latency` | Minimize document latency | HTML document delivery/response contributes to load delay. | Opportunity |
| `duplicated-javascript` | Avoid serving legacy JavaScript to modern browsers | Duplicate JS bundles or legacy delivery increase payload. | Opportunity |
| `critical-request-chains` | Preload key requests | Long critical request chains are delaying important resources. | Opportunity |
| `server-response-time` | Reduce initial server response time | The initial response from the server is slow. | Opportunity |
| `font-size` | Ensure text is legible | Text sizing/legibility issues can affect readability. | Diagnostic |

| Audit ID | Title | Measures / verifies | Type |
|---|---|---|---|
| `dom-size` | Avoid an excessive DOM size | Whether the DOM tree is excessively large or deep. | Diagnostic |
| `critical-request-chains` | Preload key requests | The chain of dependent network requests needed before first render. | Diagnostic |
| `user-timings` | User Timing marks and measures | Custom performance marks/measures recorded by the page. | Diagnostic |
| `resource-summary` | Displays a summary of page resources | Breaks down resource types, counts, and transfer sizes. | Diagnostic |
| `uses-rel-preconnect` | Preconnect to required origins | Whether preconnect could reduce connection setup latency. | Diagnostic |
| `network-rtt` | Network round trip times | Estimated RTT on the tested connection. | Diagnostic |
| `network-server-latency` | Server backend latencies | Estimated backend latency contributing to response time. | Diagnostic |
| `main-thread-tasks` | Main-thread tasks | Main-thread task breakdown during load. | Diagnostic |
| `metrics` | Metrics | Underlying timing metrics and related page-load measurements. | Diagnostic |
| `bootup-time` | JavaScript execution time | Time spent parsing, compiling, and executing JavaScript. | Diagnostic |

A few important clarifications:

- Lighthouse’s **Performance score** is based on the six metrics listed above, while the other audits are **opportunities** or **diagnostics** that explain *why* performance is good or poor.[1][5]
- Some audit IDs are easy to confuse because the human-readable labels have changed over time; the safest canonical identifier is the **audit ID** in Lighthouse JSON.[5][9]
- Lighthouse documentation confirms the Performance category exists and is focused on helping measure performance and find opportunities to speed up page loads, but the public docs do not always present the full audit list in one canonical table on the same page.[5][8]

If you want, I can next turn this into a **clean, machine-readable CSV/JSON table** with only the exact IDs, titles, and types.
